title: rating 4.0 / reviews_count 1024 can be anti-bot placeholders, not real data
summary: When Google detects automation it serves a reduced 'compact' JSON format with only name + place_id; its rating defaults to 4.0 and reviews_count to 1024. These are placeholders. The worker's DOM-extraction layer fills the real values when it can, but if you see exactly 4.0 / 1024 repeated across listings, treat them as suspect. Missing website/phone/address is also normal (not every business lists them) — don't conflate a data gap with an extraction failure.
references: docs/services/SpiderMaps/CLAUDE.md:402-413 — Full vs Compact format; 'reviews_count == 1024' and 'rating == 4.0' are placeholders from compact
docs/services/SpiderBrowser/LEARNINGS.md:217-221 — COMPACT format is expected (TLS fingerprint detection), DOM layer fills gaps
